title: Brewers Prospect Cody Ponce Sidelined with ACL Sprain Amid Ongoing MLB Trade Rumors
body:
Toronto Blue Jays Pitcher Cody Ponce Sidelined With ACL Sprain, Expected to Miss Significant Time

The Toronto Blue Jays suffered a significant setback when right-hander Cody Ponce was diagnosed with an ACL sprain, dealing a blow to the team's rotation. Ponce, who made his first big league appearance in years, was injured in his opening game and is now expected to be sidelined for a significant period. The team has officially placed Ponce on the 15-day injured list (IL) and recalled right-hander Estrada to take his place.

Ponce's injury is a major concern for the Blue Jays, who had high hopes for the right-hander's return to the big leagues. A native of California, Ponce had been working his way through the minor league system, earning a promotion to the Blue Jays' roster after a strong performance last season. His return to the big leagues was highly anticipated, and fans were eager to see him take the mound again. Unfortunately, his first outing was cut short due to injury, and now the team will have to wait even longer to see him back on the field.

The timing of Ponce's injury is particularly unfortunate for the Blue Jays, who are in the midst of a competitive season. With a strong team and a talented roster, the Blue Jays were expected to be a major contender in the American League East. However, the loss of a key pitcher like Ponce will undoubtedly put a strain on the team's rotation, forcing the Blue Jays to re-evaluate their pitching strategy. Manager John Schneider expressed his concern about Ponce's injury, stating that the team would need to find ways to compensate for the loss of his services.

While the Blue Jays' pitching staff is deep and talented, the loss of Ponce will still be felt. With Estrada recalled to take his place, the team will need to rely on its other right-handers, such as Yusei Kikuchi and Alek Manoah, to step up and fill the void. The team's bullpen will also be called upon to provide additional support, with closer Jordan Romano likely to see more action in the coming weeks.

Despite the setback, the Blue Jays remain optimistic about their chances this season. With a strong team and a talented roster, they are confident that they can overcome the loss of Ponce and continue to compete at a high level. As the team navigates the next few weeks without their injured right-hander, fans will be watching closely to see how they respond to the challenge. Will the Blue Jays be able to find a way to overcome Ponce's injury and remain a contender in the American League East? Only time will tell.

In the meantime, the Blue Jays will need to focus on finding ways to compensate for the loss of Ponce's services. With a deep and talented roster, they have the resources to do so, but it will undoubtedly require some adjustments. As the team looks to the future, they will need to rely on their other players to step up and fill the void left by Ponce's injury. With a strong team and a talented roster, the Blue Jays are confident that they can overcome this setback and remain a force to be reckoned with in the American League East.
